assertions.title: File creation preserves multipart upload semantics
assertions.observed: The required live smoke passed and printed a real file id plus a create request id after creating a file.
assertions.issues: The captured live output does not expose multipart wire details or optional expires_after handling, so the full multipart-upload contract is not directly proven by this run.

assertions.title: File content and file-processing helpers preserve retrieval behavior
assertions.observed: The required live smoke passed and printed a file-content request id after retrieving file content successfully.
assertions.issues: The live output does not directly show the binary payload bytes or exercise the wait_for_processing polling helper, so the assigned assertion is not fully proven by this run.

assertions.title: File Retrieve and Delete Preserve Metadata and Deletion Semantics
assertions.observed: The required live smoke passed and printed real retrieve and delete request ids for the created file.
assertions.issues: The captured output does not directly prove local empty-id rejection or stable metadata-field preservation, so the full retrieve/delete contract is not directly established from this log alone.

commands.command: set -a && . ./creds.txt && set +a && unset OPENAI_BASE_URL && cargo test --test live_files_smoke -- --ignored --nocapture

blockers.description: The single live smoke run is narrower than the assigned assertion scopes, so multipart wire details, wait_for_processing polling, empty-id guards, and metadata-field stability are not directly observable in the captured output.

summary: Ran the required live cargo smoke successfully and captured request IDs, but VAL-FILES-001, VAL-FILES-003, and VAL-FILES-020 remain blocked because the live output does not directly prove the full contract scope for those assertions.
